options.name: --server-name
options.description:
Name of the SQL server to connect
to.

options.name: --database-name
options.description:
(Default: xperience) Name of the database to
create.

options.name: --username
options.description:
Username used for SQL server authentication.
If not provided, windows authentication is
used to connect to the SQL server.

options.name: --password
options.description: Password used for SQL server authentication.

options.name: --admin-password
options.description:
Sets the password for the default
'administrator' account.

options.name: --hash-string-salt
options.description:
Custom salt used for the hash function to
create the macro signatures.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Indicates if verbose logging is enabled.

options.name: --recreate-existing-database
options.description:
Indicates if the database is recreated if it
already exists. Otherwise use
'--use-existing-database' option or the
installation is terminated.

options.name: --use-existing-database
options.description:
Indicates if the database should be used if
it already exists. Otherwise use
'--recreate-existing-database' option or the
installation is terminated.

options.name: --license-file
options.description:
The absolute or relative path of the license
file containing the license key for the site.

options.name: --sample-website-domain
options.description:
(Default: localhost) The domain to be
configured for the sample website.
'Localhost' will serve as the default value
if not provided. The option is ignored if no
sample website exists in the database.

options.name: --sample-email-service-domain
options.description:
The Email service domain set for the sample
email channel. Use this parameter if you want
to override the default 'dancinggoat.ai'
service domain for the sample email channel.
The parameter is ignored if no sample website
exists in the database.

options.name: --instance-friendly-name
options.description:
Represents the instance in Client Portal. If
left empty, a default numeric identifier is
used instead. Friendly names may only contain
letters, numbers, spaces, and the special
characters: .,?!|'\/-_
